task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Access the VistA database within YDBGui.
task_completion.visual_evidence: YDBGui shows an active connection to the database, displaying a list of globals or a query interface.
The global ^GMR(123.5) is entered in the search/query bar, or explicitly selected and expanded in the global list within YDBGui.

task_completion.description: View the consult service names within the ^GMR(123.5) global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data viewer in YDBGui displays records, nodes, or data values under ^GMR(123.5) that contain text names of consult services.

task_completion.description: Create or open the target text file for the audit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad, nano) is open with the file path /home/ga/Documents/consult_services_audit.txt visible in the title bar, tab, or status bar.

task_completion.description: List at least three consult service names in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows at least three distinct service names written in the file, formatted with one per line.

integrity.description: The agent used YDBGui to find the services, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the YDBGui interface to browse the database, rather than running MUMPS commands or scripts in a terminal to extract the data.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a text editor to create the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a GUI text editor or terminal-based text editor (like nano/vim) being manually edited, rather than the file being generated solely via background scripts or echo commands.

integrity.description: The services listed in the text file match those seen in YDBGui.
integrity.visual_evidence: The specific consult service names typed into the text file should correspond to the data values visible in the YDBGui ^GMR(123.5) viewer, proving the agent didn't hallucinate or guess the names.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target global is ^GMR(123.5), which represents the VistA REQUEST SERVICES file. The agent must use the YDBGui application to view this global. The expected output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/consult_services_audit.txt and contain a minimum of 3 valid consult service names extracted from the database.
